justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Event sourcing identified as appropriate but hybrid/simpler alternatives explicitly presented (CDC+audit log, temporal tables, pure ES all compared); (2) Schema evolution risk identified with detailed mitigation (explicit versioning, upcasters, governance process); (3) Snapshotting requirement identified as a dedicated component and pattern with concrete guidance (every 50 events); (4) Simpler audit-log alternative presented (CDC+audit log table, temporal tables both discussed as lower-complexity options); (5) trade_offs include clear gains and sacrifices for each decision; (6) implementation_guidance distinguishes hybrid from full event sourcing via a phased approach (Phase 1-3) with explicit migration path.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the hybrid approach — recommending event log + current-state table as the pragmatic starting point rather than defaulting to full event sourcing is exactly the nuanced guidance expected

PII/GDPR crypto-shredding risk identified as critical severity — this goes beyond the rubric requirements and demonstrates sophisticated domain awareness of the tension between immutable audit logs and right-to-erasure
Schema evolution addressed in multiple places: risks section, flexibility_points (upcasters), and pitfalls_to_avoid — thorough treatment
Three distinct alternatives compared in trade_offs (pure ES, temporal tables, CDC+audit log) with clear reasoning for each rejection
Snapshotting addressed as both an architectural component (Snapshot Service) and a risk mitigation with concrete threshold (every 50 events)
Implementation guidance is phased and actionable, with concrete file paths, SQL schema, and explicit pitfalls — distinguishes hybrid from full ES with a clear migration path between them

Output is very lengthy — the executive summary alone is excellent and concise, but the full output could be more focused. Some sections (e.g., cryptographic hash chain) add complexity beyond what the scenario required
